text:
There was an old farmer who lived alone on his farm. His home was small, but warm, built mostly of wood. He grew crops and kept chickens. There was one peculiar custom he observed daily—a feather pen.

One morning while walking his flock, he noticed his feather pen missing. Panicking, he searched everywhere. It wasn't long before he found it curled safely beneath the wheelbarrow.

It happened every morning. He couldn't help himself. He'd put it back. Why else?

One day, birds took notice and brought him some crumbs. He felt oddly enlightened. He became obsessed with crafting letters.

Soon he sent them home. Letters home. To his dog. Letters to the cows. Maybe even one to his cat. Little did he know his cat liked stories.

Then letters to neighbors.

Neighbors loved it. They even sent messages. They had cats.

His farm flourished. Stories brought people together.

Now when he died, the villagers came for his grave. They
